title: [Feature Request] Add CSV import for bulk task creation
description:
Summary
The user is requesting CSV import support to create many tasks at once during migration from another tool. This would significantly reduce manual setup time and improve onboarding for users with large existing task lists.

User Need
Users migrating to TaskPro from other tools may already have hundreds of tasks stored in CSV format and need an efficient way to bring that data into the app. Without CSV import, they must recreate tasks manually, creating major friction during onboarding and increasing the time required to adopt TaskPro.

Proposed Solution
Add CSV import for bulk task creation, with a defined import schema for common task fields and a guided import flow that helps users map columns and identify validation errors before completion. Consider supporting core fields first, such as task title, description, due date, priority, and status, then expand based on usage and migration needs.

Impact Assessment
This request appears to have high impact because it directly affects migration effort, onboarding speed, and willingness to adopt TaskPro for users with large existing task lists. Demand signal is strong based on the explicit request, the scale of data involved, and the clear time savings described by the user.

This would significantly reduce manual effort during migration from another tool and make onboarding easier for users with large existing task lists.","user_impact":"High","demand_signal":"Strong","existing_alternatives":"Current workaround is manual task entry; no other alternative was mentioned.","source":"support_email / E015"}
suggested_actions: ["Validate demand by checking for similar migration and bulk import requests","Define CSV schema and import flow for task fields and error handling","Assess implementation scope for mobile and backend support for bulk task creation"]
quality_notes: Well-structured feature ticket with a clear title, strong articulation of the user need, and a plausible proposed solution grounded in the original feedback. Priority may be slightly overstated relative to the single low-priority request, and the ticket would be stronger with clearer evidence of breadth of demand or product rationale for assigning High priority.

title: [Bug] Deadline notifications delayed by 2-3 hours
description:
Summary
Users report that TaskPro deadline notifications on Android are consistently delivered 2–3 hours after the configured reminder time, despite notifications being enabled and battery optimization being disabled. This delay has caused missed important deadlines.

Steps to Reproduce
1. Install and open TaskPro version 3.2.1 on a OnePlus 12 running Android 14.
2. Enable deadline notifications in the app and ensure system notifications are allowed.
3. Confirm battery optimization is disabled for TaskPro.
4. Create or edit a task with a deadline reminder set for a specific time.
5. Wait until the scheduled reminder time passes.
6. Observe that the notification arrives 2–3 hours after the configured deadline/reminder time.

Expected Behavior
Deadline notifications should arrive at the scheduled reminder or deadline time.

Actual Behavior
Deadline notifications consistently arrive 2–3 hours late, causing users to miss deadlines.

Environment
- Device: OnePlus 12
- OS: Android 14
- App Version: 3.2.1
- Source: support_email / E014
technical_details: {"affected_component":"Notifications","platform_details":{"device":"OnePlus 12","os":"Android 14","app_version":"3.2.1"},"reported_pattern":"Consistent 2-3 hour delay in deadline notification delivery","user_validations":["System notifications enabled","Battery optimization disabled"],"suspected_areas":["Notification scheduling","Time handling/timezone logic","Alarm/worker execution on Android 14","OEM background restrictions on OnePlus devices"]}
suggested_actions: ["Investigate notification scheduling and time handling on Android 14, especially for OnePlus devices.","Review alarm/worker execution logic for timezone, Doze, and OEM background restrictions despite battery optimization being disabled.","Attempt reproduction on a OnePlus 12 with app version 3.2.1 and collect notification delivery logs."]
quality_notes: Strong bug ticket with a clear, correctly prefixed title, solid environment details, and actionable reproduction steps that align well with the original feedback. Minor gap: the repro steps infer task creation and waiting behavior not explicitly stated by the user, so adding any exact reminder configuration or frequency data from logs would make it even stronger.

title: [Complaint] Mobile app lacks parity and feels subpar vs web
description:
Summary
The user reports strong dissatisfaction with the mobile app compared with the web experience, citing missing features, a cluttered interface, and poor perceived product quality. They also highlight that the current subscription price creates an expectation of better platform parity.

Issue Description
- The mobile app is described as significantly worse than the web version.
- Key features appear to be missing on mobile.
- The mobile UI is perceived as cluttered.
- The user feels the mobile experience was treated as an afterthought.
- The complaint is amplified by the $9.99/month subscription cost and expectation of consistency across platforms.

User Impact
- Reduced satisfaction and trust in the product’s cross-platform experience.
- Users may avoid or abandon the mobile app due to perceived lower usability and incomplete functionality.
- Subscription value perception is negatively affected, increasing churn risk.

Suggested Resolution
- Review feature parity gaps between web and mobile and prioritize the most visible missing capabilities.
- Conduct a UX assessment of the mobile interface to identify clutter and simplify core workflows.
- Align mobile roadmap and messaging with user expectations for paid subscriptions.
- Consider communicating planned parity improvements to affected users.
suggested_actions: ["Audit web vs mobile feature parity gaps","Review mobile UX for clutter and workflow friction","Assess churn/value perception risk for paid mobile users","Communicate roadmap or parity improvements to users"]
quality_notes: Strong ticket that accurately reflects the original complaint and clearly captures the user impact and likely follow-up areas. To improve further, it should identify specific missing mobile features or workflows and clarify whether Medium priority is based on broader trend data versus this single low-priority complaint.

title: [Feature Request] Add manager dashboard for team-wide progress
description:
Summary
The user is requesting a manager dashboard that provides a centralized view of all team members’ task progress. Their team of 15 currently has to open each individual board separately, creating unnecessary overhead for managers.

User Need
Managers overseeing multiple team members need a single place to monitor task progress across the team at a glance. The current workflow of checking each person’s board individually is time-consuming and does not scale well for larger teams.

Proposed Solution
Add a manager progress dashboard that summarizes task status and progress across all team members in one view. The dashboard should support high-level progress summaries, filters, and drill-down into individual team members’ work so managers can quickly identify status and follow up where needed.

Impact Assessment
This request appears to have high impact because it addresses a clear efficiency pain point for team management and is coming from a team using TaskPro across 15 users. Demand signal is strong based on the explicit request, the team-management context, and the significant time cost of the current workaround.

title: [Bug] App freezes creating task with due date on Android 14
description:
Summary
The app becomes unresponsive for 15–20 seconds whenever the user creates a new task with a due date. The issue occurs consistently on a Google Pixel 7a running Android 14 in TaskPro version 3.2.0 and requires force closing the app.

Steps to Reproduce
1. Open TaskPro on a Google Pixel 7a running Android 14.
2. Start creating a new task.
3. Add or select a due date for the task.
4. Attempt to save or continue creating the task.
5. Observe the app freezing for 15–20 seconds and requiring a force close.

Expected Behavior
Creating a new task with a due date should complete smoothly without freezing, and the app should remain responsive.

Actual Behavior
The app consistently freezes for about 15–20 seconds when creating a new task with a due date, and the user must force close and reopen the app.

Environment
- Device: Google Pixel 7a
- OS: Android 14
- App Version: 3.2.0
- Source: support_email / E011
suggested_actions: ["Reproduce the issue on a Google Pixel 7a with Android 14 using app version 3.2.0.","Investigate task creation and due date picker logic for UI thread blocking or performance regressions.","Review crash, ANR, and performance logs around task save flow to identify the freeze source."]
quality_notes: Strong bug ticket: the title is clear and correctly prefixed, and the description includes expected/actual behavior, repro steps, and environment that align closely with the original feedback. Priority is reasonable for a consistent app-freeze issue, though the added repro detail goes slightly beyond the raw report and could be validated during triage.

title: [Bug] Tasks not syncing between iPhone app and web app
description:
Summary
User reports that tasks created on an iPhone 15 running TaskPro 3.2.1 do not appear in the web app on Chrome/Windows 11, and tasks created in the web app do not appear on the phone. Sync reportedly worked until about a week ago, and logging out and back in on both devices did not resolve the issue.

Steps to Reproduce
1. Log in to TaskPro on an iPhone 15 running iOS 17.2 with app version 3.2.1.
2. Create a new task on the phone.
3. Log in to the TaskPro web app on a Windows 11 laptop using Chrome.
4. Check whether the newly created task appears on the web app.
5. Create a different task in the web app on the laptop.
6. Return to the phone app and check whether the web-created task appears there.

Expected Behavior
Tasks created on one device should sync and appear on the other device across mobile and web.

Actual Behavior
Tasks created on the iPhone do not appear on the laptop web app, and tasks created on the laptop do not appear on the iPhone.

Environment
- Mobile device: iPhone 15
- Mobile OS: iOS 17.2
- Mobile app version: 3.2.1
- Web environment: Chrome on Windows 11
- Troubleshooting already attempted: Logged out and back in on both devices
- Timing: Issue began approximately one week ago after previously working correctly
technical_details: {"affected_component":"Sync","platform_details":{"device":"iPhone 15 and laptop","os":"iOS 17.2 and Windows 11","app_version":"3.2.1 on phone, web app on Chrome"}}
suggested_actions: ["Investigate sync service/backend changes from the past week for regressions affecting cross-platform task updates.","Review client and server sync logs for iOS app version 3.2.1 and web sessions in Chrome on Windows 11.","Attempt to reproduce using iPhone iOS 17.2 and web app on Chrome to confirm whether re-authentication or account state affects sync."]
quality_notes: Strong bug ticket that closely matches the original feedback and adds clear reproduction steps, expected vs. actual behavior, environment details, and reasonable technical next steps. Priority is appropriate for a cross-platform sync regression affecting core functionality, so this is ready for the backlog.

title: [Feature Request] Provide API access for task data integrations
description:
Summary
The user is requesting API access so their company can integrate TaskPro task data with internal reporting tools. This request comes from an organization using TaskPro across 50+ employees and includes explicit willingness to upgrade to a higher plan for this capability.

User Need
Business and enterprise customers need a reliable way to access task data programmatically so they can connect TaskPro with internal reporting and analytics workflows. Without API access, teams must rely on manual processes or cannot incorporate TaskPro data into existing internal systems.

Proposed Solution
Provide API access for task data, starting with a scoped roadmap for read access to tasks and reporting-related data. Consider packaging API functionality within higher-tier or enterprise plans, and define roadmap communications for customers asking about API availability and timeline.

Impact Assessment
This feature appears to have high impact because it supports enterprise integration workflows, improves TaskPro's suitability for larger organizations, and may create upsell opportunities for higher-priced plans. Demand signal is strong based on the customer size, clear integration use case, and stated willingness to pay more for the feature.

Issue Description

User Impact
The customer incurred an unexpected duplicate billing charge totaling an extra $9.99 and is expressing frustration, which may affect retention and trust.

Suggested Resolution
Review the billing records for the listed transactions, confirm whether a duplicate charge occurred, issue a refund for the duplicate transaction if validated, and communicate the resolution promptly to the customer.

Steps to Reproduce

2. Open the app dashboard or projects view.
3. Observe the projects and tasks list immediately after app load.

Expected Behavior
All previously created projects and their associated tasks should remain visible and accessible after opening the app.

Actual Behavior
All projects appear to be missing, including over 50 active projects and hundreds of tasks, indicating possible data loss or a severe sync/display failure.

Environment
- Source: support_email / E005
- Plan: Enterprise
- Device: MacBook Pro M3
- Browser: Chrome
- OS: Unknown
- App Version: Unknown
- Affected Component: Projects/Tasks
suggested_actions: ["Immediately investigate backend data integrity, account-level project and task records, and any recent sync or deletion events for the affected enterprise account.","Check for web app regressions specific to Chrome on MacBook Pro M3 and determine whether this is a display/sync issue versus permanent data loss.","Preserve relevant logs, contact the customer promptly, and attempt account data restoration from backups if records were deleted or corrupted."]

title: [Feature Request] Add task dependencies for project workflows
description:
Summary
The user is requesting task dependency support so one task can be blocked until another is completed. This would improve TaskPro for project management workflows that require tasks to be completed in sequence.

User Need
Users managing multi-step projects need a way to define dependencies between tasks, such as preventing Task B from starting until Task A is complete. Without this capability, users must track sequencing manually, which makes project coordination harder and increases the risk of work starting out of order.

Proposed Solution
Add task dependency management that allows users to link tasks with prerequisite relationships. An initial implementation could support basic finish-to-start dependencies, visually indicate blocked tasks, and prevent dependent tasks from being marked as in progress until prerequisite tasks are completed.

Impact Assessment
This request has high user impact because it supports a more advanced project management use case and helps users organize task sequences with less manual oversight. Demand signal appears strong from the explicit workflow example provided, and the feature could expand TaskPro’s usefulness for teams and individuals managing structured projects.

suggested_actions: ["Validate demand by reviewing similar project-management feature requests and customer segments","Define dependency types and UX flows, such as blocking tasks until prerequisite completion","Assess implementation scope across task model, scheduling logic, and mobile UI"]

title: [Bug] Login fails with 'Authentication failed' after update 3.2.1

Steps to Reproduce
1. Open TaskPro on a Samsung Galaxy S24 Ultra running Android 14.
2. Ensure the app is updated to version 3.2.1.
3. Navigate to the login screen.
4. Enter valid account credentials.
5. Tap the login button.
6. Observe the authentication result on either WiFi or mobile data.

Expected Behavior
User should be able to log into the account successfully when entering correct credentials.

Actual Behavior
The app displays an 'Authentication failed' error and prevents login even when correct credentials are entered.

Environment
- Device: Samsung Galaxy S24 Ultra
- OS: Android 14
- App Version: 3.2.1
- Network Conditions: WiFi and mobile data
- Affected Component: Login
- Source: support_email / E003
suggested_actions: ["Reproduce the issue on Android 14 with app version 3.2.1 using valid test accounts.","Review recent authentication and login-related changes introduced in version 3.2.1.","Check backend auth logs and client error handling to identify whether the failure is app-side or server-side."]

Priority is appropriate for a login-blocking issue, and the ticket is actionable for engineering without further clarification.

title: [Feature Request] Add dark mode theme for reduced eye strain
description:
Summary
The user is requesting a dark mode theme for TaskPro to reduce eye strain during late-night use. They note that the current bright white interface is uncomfortable and that competing apps already offer this capability.

User Need
Users who work in low-light or nighttime settings need a darker interface option that is more comfortable to view for extended periods. This request reflects both a usability need and a competitive expectation, since similar apps already provide dark mode.

Proposed Solution
Add a dark mode theme option for the app interface, ideally with support for both a manual toggle and optional system theme matching. Review the existing theme architecture, validate accessible dark color palettes across key screens, and consolidate similar dark mode requests to assess overall demand.

Impact Assessment
This feature appears to have high user impact because it addresses eye strain, improves comfort for nighttime usage, and helps TaskPro meet common market expectations. Demand signal is strong based on the explicit request and competitor comparison, making this a strong candidate for roadmap prioritization.

description:

title: [Feature Request] Add built-in Pomodoro timer to task view
description:
Summary
The user is requesting a built-in Pomodoro timer within the task view to support focused work sessions without leaving TaskPro. This would make it easier to use time-boxed productivity techniques directly alongside task execution.

User Need
The user wants an integrated focus timer so they can stay in the task view while working through Pomodoro-style sessions. No workaround was mentioned, suggesting a gap in support for users who rely on structured focus intervals.

Proposed Solution
Add a Pomodoro timer directly to the task view, with an MVP focused on core timer controls such as start, pause, reset, and configurable work/break intervals. The implementation should consider in-app visibility, notifications for session transitions, and whether session progress should be associated with the active task.

Impact Assessment
This request appears to have medium user impact with a strong demand signal based on the feedback provided. A built-in Pomodoro timer could improve TaskPro’s usefulness for focus-oriented workflows and reduce the need for users to switch to separate timer apps during work sessions.

title: [Feature Request] Add Siri Shortcuts for voice task creation
description:

The user is requesting Siri Shortcuts support so they can create tasks in TaskPro using voice commands. This would improve speed and convenience for capturing tasks while hands-free or on the go.

User Need
The user needs a faster, more accessible way to add tasks without manually opening the app and typing. Voice-based task creation would help in situations where the user is driving, walking, or otherwise unable to interact directly with the app.

Proposed Solution
Add Siri Shortcuts support for task creation on iOS, potentially through Siri Shortcuts and App Intents integration. An MVP could focus on a simple flow that lets users invoke Siri to create a new task with spoken task content.

Impact Assessment
This request has medium user impact with a strong demand signal, and no workaround was mentioned in the feedback. Supporting Siri-based task capture could improve usability for iOS users and strengthen TaskPro’s value in quick-capture and accessibility-focused workflows.
